steps.original_output:
### 1. Facts given in the task
• The task asks for the identification of a specific two‐word type of model.  
• It states that both Manash Pratim Kashyap’s and PS Fader’s studies in customer retention (published between 2018–2019) used this model in common.  
• The answer must contain no punctuation and must be exactly two words that name the model type.

### 2. Facts to look up
• Publication details for Manash Pratim Kashyap’s customer retention study(ies) from 2018–2019 – to identify the model type it employed.  
  – Where to find: Academic databases such as Google Scholar, IEEE Xplore, or journal websites.  
• Publication details for PS Fader’s customer retention study(ies) from 2018–2019 – to determine the model type it used.  
  – Where to find: The same academic databases (e.g., Google Scholar, JSTOR, publisher websites).  
• Information on common models used in customer retention research during that period – including the names of the models, methodologies, and possible two-word candidates.  
  – Where to find: Review articles, textbooks in marketing analytics, or academic literature reviews on customer retention modeling.

### 3. Facts to derive
• By comparing the methodologies from the above studies, derive which specific two‐word model appears in both.  
• From the gathered publication details, logically infer that the common model type (e.g., “negative binomial” or “markov chain” – as candidates) is the one that both studies share.  
• Verification through cross‐referencing the model descriptions and methodologies of both papers so that the final answer is based on confirmed evidence from the literature.
